overview.service: Virtual chronic disease prevention and management programs for diabetes, hypertension, and musculoskeletal conditions
overview.consumer_base: Employer health plan members and Medicare Advantage participants across the US
overview.partnership_rationale: Omada Health is a CDC-recognized digital diabetes prevention program provider with peer-reviewed clinical evidence and an active research agenda, creating partnership opportunities with UNC's behavioral health, chronic disease prevention, and digital health informatics programs.
overview.contact_reasoning: Omada publishes peer-reviewed outcomes research and has collaborated with academic institutions on clinical studies evaluating its digital behavior change programs, providing a model for UNC partnership.

unc_connection.donation_history: No confirmed direct donation history to UNC on record. Verify via UNC Office of Development.

talking_points.detail: Omada's digital diabetes prevention program is fully recognized by the CDC's National DPP framework and covered by Medicare, providing an evidence-based digital intervention platform that UNC's prevention research programs can study for implementation and scalability.

Omada has published multiple peer-reviewed studies demonstrating significant weight loss and HbA1c reduction outcomes in its enrolled populations, establishing clinical credibility that supports academic research collaboration.

Omada's platform generates longitudinal engagement, biometric, and behavioral data across enrolled members, creating a research resource for UNC's digital health engagement, health coaching, and behavior change informatics research.

Omada has expanded beyond diabetes prevention into hypertension management and musculoskeletal care, creating research opportunities across a broader chronic disease portfolio aligned with UNC's multi-condition chronic disease research programs.

Omada has studied disparities in digital chronic disease program engagement across racial and socioeconomic groups, creating a research agenda on equitable digital health program design that aligns with UNC's health equity priorities.

references.title: Effect of a Digital Health Diabetes Prevention Program on Weight Loss
